KPLC RELAYED A REPORT OF DAMAGE FROM A POSSIBLE TORNADO ON THE CORNER OF EVERGREEN AND VERRET RD OUTSIDE OF WESTLAKE. DAMAGE INCLUDED MULTIPLE TREES DOWN, SHEET METAL LIFTED OFF A BARN, AN ALUMINUM CARPORT BLOWN OVER AND CARRIED APPROX 300 FT. SEVERAL PUBLIC SOURCES ALSO REPORTED SIMILAR DAMAGE IN THE AREA.

ALONG EVERGREEN STREET BETWEEN SULPHUR AND WESTLAKE...A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN...BLOWING TREES DOWN AND DAMAGING SEVERAL HOMES WHEN TREES FELL ON THE ROOF. THIS STORM CONTINUED TO THE NORTHEAST AND HIT SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES NEAR THE GOLF COURSE IN WESTLAKE BEFORE IT MOVED INTO MOSS BLUFF WHERE IT RIPPED SHINGLES OFF A COUPLE OF HOMES...DESTROYED A STORAGE BUILDING...BLEW A FENCE DOWN...AND KNOCKED DOWN SOME TREES. THIS TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 5.7 MILES AND WAS 25 YARDS WIDE.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF 1 TORNADO TOUCHDOWN JUST SOUTH OF LAKE ARTHUR. THE TORNADO TRAVELED NORTH THROUGH THE CITY OF LAKE ARTHUR ON A TWO MILE LONG PATH. PATH WIDTH WAS 40 YARDS. AN ESTIMATED 20-30 HOMES WERE DAMAGED. DAMAGE INCLUDED THE PEELING BACK OF ROOVES, BARNS DESTROYED, TREES DOWNED AND ANTENNAS BLOWN OFF THE TOP OF A WATER TOWER.

*** 1 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF 1 TORNADO TOUCHDOWN ONE MILE SOUTH OF GUEYDAN ALONG LA 91. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 3 MILES WITH A WIDTH OF 40 YARDS. DAMAGE INCLUDED A TIED DOWN MOBILE HOME BEING ROLLED UPSIDE DOWN.THERE WAS SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE TO FIVE HOMES AS WELL AS CITY HALL. ABOUT 20 HOMES RECEIVED MINOR DAMAGE. THE TORNADO PASSED OVER A CEMETARY WHERE IT KNOCKED DOWN. GRAVESTONES. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED AND NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWNED. ONE MINOR INJURY OCCURED.

DAMAGE INCLUDED A TIED DOWN MOBILE HOME BEING ROLLED UPSIDE DOWN.THERE WAS SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE TO FIVE HOMES AS WELL AS CITY HALL. ABOUT 20 HOMES RECEIVED MINOR DAMAGE. THE TORNADO PASSED OVER A CEMETARY WHERE IT KNOCKED DOWN GRAVESTONES. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED AND NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWNED.

A TRAMPOLINE FLEW 0.17 MILE IN THE AIR...AND HIT THE SIDE OF A MOBILE HOME ON AVONDALE STREET...BREAKING SEVERAL WINDOWS AND A SLIDING GLASS DOOR...AS WELL AS MOVING THE OUTDOOR STAIRWAYS AT LEAST 5 FEET AND DAMAGING THE AIR CONDITIONER AND PART OF THE ROOF. AT LEAST THREE OTHER MOBILE HOMES RECEIVED MINOR WIND DAMAGE SUCH AS LOST SHINGLES OR LOST SIDING.ONE TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN. ONE PORTABLE STORAGE BUILDING WAS DESTROYED. TWO CAMPING TRAILERS WERE BLOWN OFF THEIR BLOCKS.

STATE POLICE REPORT DAMAGE TO A BARN AND TREES DOWN NEAR PITKIN AS THE RESULT OF A POSSIBLE TORNADO. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMS AN EF 0 TORNADO TOUCHDOWN THREE MILES EAST OF THE TOWN OF PITKIN. THE TORNADO PRODUCED A DAMAGE PATH TWO MILES LONG AND 200 YARDS WIDE. THE TORNADO SNAPPED NUMEROUS PINE TREES ALONG HIGHWAY 113. A TREE FELL ON A HOUSE CAUSING MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. THE TORNADO ALSO PARTIALLY REMOVED A ROOF FROM A GARAGE.

*** 1 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF 2 TORNADO TOUCHDOWN IN THE TOWN OF TIOGA. THE TORNADO PRODUCED A DAMAGE PATH FOUR MILES LONG AND 1/2 MILE WIDE. THE TORNADO DAMAGED APPROXIMATELY 43 HOMES AS WELL AS TIOGA HIGH SCHOOL AND INVENTORY AT A TRUCKING COMPANY. ONE MINOR INJURY OCCURED WITH THE INJURED PARTY REFUSING TREATMENT.
NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTS A 5TH WHEEL FLIPPED OVER AT 118 WOODCREEK LOOP. NUMEROUS PINE TREES AND POWERLINES ARE ALSO REPORTED TO BE DOWN.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF 2 TORNADO TOUCHDOWN IN THE CITY OF ALEXANDRIA. THE TORNADO PRODUCED A DAMAGE PATH 2 MILES LONG AND 200 YARDS WIDE. LARGE PIECES OF ROOF MATERIAL WERE BLOWN OFF OF BUILDINGS IN JACKSON SQUARE. THE TORNADO BLEW A NON MOVING CHEVY COBALT OFF THE ROAD AND INTO A YARD. SOME DAMAGE TO HOMES AND VEHICLES OCCURED AS A RESULT OF TREES FALLING ONTO THEM.
ALEXANDRIA ASOS REPORTED A 61 MPH WIND GUST.

RAPIDES PARISH SHERIFF REPORTS 25-30 HOMES DAMAGED WITH NUMEROUS INJURIES. EMERGENCY PERSONNEL ARE HAVING DIFFICULTY REACHING VICTIMS DUE TO THE DAMAGE.

RAPIDES PARISH SHERIFF REPORTS 25-30 HOMES DAMAGED WITH NUMEROUS INJURIES ON RIGOLETTE RD AND SHREVEPORT HWY. EMERGENCY PERSONNEL ARE HAVING DIFFICULTY REACHING VICTIMS DUE TO THE DAMAGE. A MAN WAS REPORTEDLY TRAPPED INSIDE A FLIPPED OVER MOBILE HOME.
